what: Run the end-to-end mystery caller workflow — mysterycall_run_workflow
title: Run the end-to-end mystery caller workflow — mysterycall_run_workflow
text: helper orchestrates core steps required prepare execute mystery caller campaign. stitches together roster creation, NPI validation, call sheet preparation, workload splitting, Phase 2 hygiene checks teams can focus execution instead plumbing.

title: Run the end-to-end mystery caller workflow — mysterycall_run_workflow
code: mysterycall_run_workflow(   taxonomy_terms = NULL,   name_data = NULL,   phase1_data,   lab_assistant_names,   output_directory,   phase2_data,   phase2_output_directory = output_directory,   quality_check_path,   phase1_output_directory = output_directory,   split_insurance_order = c("Medicaid", "Blue Cross/Blue Shield"),   phase2_required_strings = c(     "physician_information", "able_to_contact_office", "are_we_including",     "reason_for_exclusions", "appointment_date", "number_of_transfers",     "call_time", "hold_time", "notes", "person_completing",     "state", "npi", "name"   ),   phase2_standard_names = c(     "physician_info", "contact_office", "included_in_study",     "exclusion_reasons", "appt_date", "transfer_count",     "call_duration", "hold_duration", "notes", "completed_by",     "state", "npi", "name"   ),   npi_search_args = list(),   all_states = NULL,   taxonomy_states = NULL,   verbose = interactive(),   npi_progress_observer = NULL )

title: Run the end-to-end mystery caller workflow — mysterycall_run_workflow
text: taxonomy_terms Character vector taxonomy descriptions pass mysterycall_search_taxonomy(). Set NULL skip taxonomy-based searches. name_data Optional data frame containing first last columns use mysterycall_search_and_process_npi(). Provide NULL skip name-based searches. phase1_data Data frame holding Phase 1 calling roster information pass mysterycall_clean_phase1(). lab_assistant_names Character vector caller names used splitting cleaned roster via mysterycall_split_and_save(). Must contain least two entries. output_directory Directory mysterycall_split_and_save() write complete per-caller workbooks. phase2_data Data frame file path consumed mysterycall_clean_phase2(). phase2_output_directory Directory Phase 2 exports written. Defaults output_directory. quality_check_path File path mysterycall_save_quality_table() write quality check CSV. phase1_output_directory Directory mysterycall_clean_phase1() write cleaned Phase 1 CSV. Defaults output_directory. split_insurance_order Ordering passed mysterycall_split_and_save()'s insurance_order argument. Defaults c("Medicaid", "Blue Cross/Blue Shield"). phase2_required_strings Character vector substrings used standardizing Phase 2 column names via mysterycall_clean_phase2(). phase2_standard_names Replacement names corresponding phase2_required_strings. npi_search_args Named list additional arguments forwarded mysterycall_search_and_process_npi(). all_states Optional character vector states supply mysterycall_not_contacted_states(). taxonomy_states Optional character vector two-letter state abbreviations passed mysterycall_search_taxonomy() restrict taxonomy searches specific states. Set NULL search states. verbose Logical. TRUE, print stage updates console running workflow. Defaults interactive(). npi_progress_observer Optional callback receives progress updates mysterycall_search_and_process_npi(). invoked payload function's progress_callback argument.

title: Run the end-to-end mystery caller workflow — mysterycall_run_workflow
text: list containing intermediate artifacts workflow stage: roster, validated_roster, cleaned_phase1, cleaned_phase2, coverage_summary, quality_check_table, workflow_summary data frame documenting row counts, retention rates, output paths audit transparency.

title: Run the mystery caller workflow with structured logging — mysterycall_run_workflow_logged
text: taxonomy_terms Character vector taxonomy descriptions pass mysterycall_search_taxonomy(). Set NULL skip taxonomy-based searches. name_data Optional data frame containing first last columns use mysterycall_search_and_process_npi(). Provide NULL skip name-based searches. phase1_data Data frame holding Phase 1 calling roster information pass mysterycall_clean_phase1(). lab_assistant_names Character vector caller names used splitting cleaned roster via mysterycall_split_and_save(). Must contain least two entries. output_directory Directory mysterycall_split_and_save() write complete per-caller workbooks. phase2_data Data frame file path consumed mysterycall_clean_phase2(). phase2_output_directory Directory Phase 2 exports written. Defaults output_directory. quality_check_path File path mysterycall_save_quality_table() write quality check CSV. phase1_output_directory Directory mysterycall_clean_phase1() write cleaned Phase 1 CSV. Defaults output_directory. split_insurance_order Ordering passed mysterycall_split_and_save()'s insurance_order argument. Defaults c("Medicaid", "Blue Cross/Blue Shield"). phase2_required_strings Character vector substrings used standardizing Phase 2 column names via mysterycall_clean_phase2(). phase2_standard_names Replacement names corresponding phase2_required_strings. npi_search_args Named list additional arguments forwarded mysterycall_search_and_process_npi(). all_states Optional character vector states supply mysterycall_not_contacted_states(). taxonomy_states Optional character vector two-letter state abbreviations forwarded mysterycall_search_taxonomy() states argument. NULL (default) search national capped 1,200 records per taxonomy term. Pass 50 abbreviations bypass cap large specialties. npi_progress_observer Optional callback receives progress updates mysterycall_search_and_process_npi(). invoked payload progress_callback argument function. log_file Optional path write plain-text log file. NULL (default), timestamped file created inside output_directory. skip_preflight Logical. TRUE, skip preflight validation step. Defaults FALSE.

title: Run the mystery caller workflow with structured logging — mysterycall_run_workflow_logged
text: Invisibly, named list returned mysterycall_run_workflow(). Key elements include cleaned_phase1 cleaned_phase2 (data frames), excluded_records (data frame), workflow_summary (stage-level counts). See mysterycall_run_workflow() complete structure.

title: Safe anti join with over-exclusion guard — mysterycall_safe_anti_join
text: left data frame. rows preserved. right data frame. lookup / enrichment table. Character vector dplyr::join_by() specifying join keys. Required; use named vector cross-column mapping (c("npi" = "provider_npi")). label_left, label_right Character scalars used messages audit report. max_matched Numeric [0, 1]. Maximum fraction left rows may matched (thus excluded). Default NULL -> reads JOIN_MAX_MATCHED env var 1.0. write_report Logical. Write one-row CSV audit record. Default FALSE. report_prefix Character scalar. Filename prefix audit CSV.

text: data frame: unmatched subset left.

title: Safe anti join with over-exclusion guard — mysterycall_safe_anti_join

title: Safe anti join with over-exclusion guard — mysterycall_safe_anti_join
code: all_physicians <- data.frame(npi = c("1","2","3","4"), stringsAsFactors = FALSE) retired        <- data.frame(npi = c("2","4"),         stringsAsFactors = FALSE)  active <- mysterycall_safe_anti_join(   left         = all_physicians,   right        = retired,   by           = "npi",   label_left   = "all_physicians",   label_right  = "retired_list",   max_matched  = 0.60,   write_report = FALSE ) #> Anti join: all_physicians kept 2/4 (excluded 50.0%)

code: mysterycall_safe_inner_join(   left,   right,   by,   expect_unique_both = TRUE,   label_left = "left",   label_right = "right",   min_coverage = NULL,   max_duplication = NULL,   suffix = c(".x", ".y"),   write_report = FALSE,   report_prefix = "join_inner" )

title: Safe inner join with cardinality checking — mysterycall_safe_inner_join
text: left data frame. rows preserved. right data frame. lookup / enrichment table. Character vector dplyr::join_by() specifying join keys. Required; use named vector cross-column mapping (c("npi" = "provider_npi")). expect_unique_both Logical. TRUE (default) keys must unique sides. label_left, label_right Character scalars used messages audit report. min_coverage Numeric [0, 1]. Default 0.90. max_duplication Numeric >= 0. Maximum allowed output/input row ratio. Default 1.02 (reads JOIN_MAX_DUPLICATION env var). suffix Character vector length 2. Suffixes conflicting column names. Default c(".x", ".y"). write_report Logical. Write one-row CSV audit record. Default FALSE. report_prefix Character scalar. Filename prefix audit CSV.

title: Safe inner join with cardinality checking — mysterycall_safe_inner_join
text: data frame: inner-join result.

title: Safe inner join with cardinality checking — mysterycall_safe_inner_join
text: Coverage measured nrow(semi_join(left, right)) / nrow(left) avoid double-counting many--many multiplication.

title: Safe inner join with cardinality checking — mysterycall_safe_inner_join
code: physicians <- data.frame(npi = c("1", "2", "3"), specialty = c("OB", "GYN", "RE"),                          stringsAsFactors = FALSE) claims     <- data.frame(npi = c("1", "2"), year = c(2020L, 2020L),                          stringsAsFactors = FALSE)  result <- mysterycall_safe_inner_join(   left         = physicians,   right        = claims,   by           = "npi",   label_left   = "physicians",   label_right  = "claims",   min_coverage = 0.50,   write_report = FALSE ) #> Join coverage: physicians 2/3 matched (66.7%)

what: Safe left join with coverage validation — mysterycall_safe_left_join
title: Safe left join with coverage validation — mysterycall_safe_left_join
text: Wraps dplyr::left_join() : Key-type harmonisation (prevents silent 0-match joins type coercion RDS Parquet round-trips). Right-side uniqueness assertion (blocks accidental many--many fan-outs). Coverage threshold enforcement – stops fewer min_coverage left rows find match. Optional CSV audit report written JOIN_REPORT_DIR env var getOption("mysterycall.join_report_dir", tempdir()).

title: Safe left join with coverage validation — mysterycall_safe_left_join

title: Safe left join with coverage validation — mysterycall_safe_left_join
text: left data frame. rows preserved. right data frame. lookup / enrichment table. Character vector dplyr::join_by() specifying join keys. Required; use named vector cross-column mapping (c("npi" = "provider_npi")). expect_unique_right Logical. TRUE (default) right-side keys must unique; stops duplicates detected. label_left, label_right Character scalars used messages audit report. min_coverage Numeric [0, 1]. Minimum fraction left rows must find match. Default 0.98 (reads JOIN_MIN_COVERAGE env var). max_duplication Numeric >= 0. Maximum allowed output/input row ratio. Default 1.02 (reads JOIN_MAX_DUPLICATION env var). suffix Character vector length 2. Suffixes conflicting column names. Default c(".x", ".y"). write_report Logical. Write one-row CSV audit record. Default FALSE. report_prefix Character scalar. Filename prefix audit CSV.

title: Safe left join with coverage validation — mysterycall_safe_left_join
text: data frame containing rows left joined matching rows right. Columns tables included; name conflicts gain suffix extensions. Stops informative error : left right data frame; columns absent; coverage falls min_coverage; output rows exceed max_duplication times input rows. write_report = TRUE, one-row CSV audit record written working directory using report_prefix filename stem.

title: Safe left join with coverage validation — mysterycall_safe_left_join

what: Search and Process NPI Numbers — mysterycall_search_and_process_npi
title: Search and Process NPI Numbers — mysterycall_search_and_process_npi
text: Searches NPI registry provider name, filters taxonomy credentials, accumulates results across batches.  Supports resume capability interrupted runs (via resume = TRUE), structured logging CSV plain-text files, optional progress callbacks long-running workflows.  Failed searches automatically retried (3 attempts) exponential backoff.

title: Search and Process NPI Numbers — mysterycall_search_and_process_npi
code: mysterycall_search_and_process_npi(   data,   enumeration_type = "ind",   limit = NULL,   country_code = "US",   filter_credentials = c("MD", "DO"),   save_chunk_size = 10,   dest_dir = NULL,   file_format = NULL,   accumulate_path = NULL,   resume = FALSE,   progress_log = NULL,   notify = TRUE,   progress_callback = NULL,   heartbeat_seconds = NULL,   progress_log_format = c("text", "csv") )

title: Search and Process NPI Numbers — mysterycall_search_and_process_npi
text: data data frame columns first last containing provider names search. enumeration_type enumeration type NPI search (e.g., "ind", "org", ""). Default "ind". limit maximum number search results request name pair. Default 5. country_code Filter "US". filter_credentials character vector containing credentials filter NPI results. Default c("MD", ""). save_chunk_size number results save per chunk. Default 10. dest_dir Destination directory save chunked results. Default NULL (files written). Files written using format specified file_format. file_format Optional override controlling whether chunked accumulated results saved "csv" "parquet". NULL, format defaults CSV unless accumulate_path ends ".parquet". accumulate_path Optional output path accumulates every non-empty result. provided, results appended successful lookup using format inferred file_format file extension. resume Logical. TRUE accumulate_path already exists, names processed previously (based search_term column) skipped. progress_log Optional file path used log progress updates. log file appended created automatically exist. notify Logical. TRUE, play notification sound processing completes (requires optional beepr package). Defaults TRUE. progress_callback Optional function invoked named list time progress updated. list contains fields event, timestamp, search_term, rows, index, total. heartbeat_seconds Optional numeric value specifying frequently (seconds) emit "still processing" heartbeat update iterating names. Set NULL disable heartbeat. progress_log_format Either "text" (default) append human readable lines progress_log "csv" write structured entries columns timestamp, event, search_term, rows, detail.

title: Search and Process NPI Numbers — mysterycall_search_and_process_npi
text: data frame 8 columns: npi Character. 10-digit NPI identifier; deduplicated within results. first_name Character. Provider first name NPI registry. last_name Character. Provider last name NPI registry. middle_name Character. Provider middle name; NA absent. credential Character. Degree credential string (e.g., "MD", ""). taxonomies_desc Character. Taxonomy classification NPI registry. search_term Character. first last name string looked . resume_row_index Integer. Row number input data; disambiguates providers identical names. Rows deduplicated NPI sorted last_name, first_name. resume = TRUE accumulate_path already exists, previously processed names skipped new results appended file. Returns empty data frame columns matches pass credential taxonomy filters.

title: Search and Process NPI Numbers — mysterycall_search_and_process_npi
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() df <- data.frame(first = "John", last = "Doe") results <- mysterycall_search_and_process_npi(df) }

what: Search NPI Database by Taxonomy — mysterycall_search_taxonomy
title: Search NPI Database by Taxonomy — mysterycall_search_taxonomy
text: function searches NPI Database healthcare providers based taxonomy description. NPI API returns 1\,200 records per query. retrieve providers large specialty, supply character vector state abbreviations via states argument; function loop state combine results, effectively bypassing 1\,200-record cap.

title: Search NPI Database by Taxonomy — mysterycall_search_taxonomy
code: mysterycall_search_taxonomy(   taxonomy_to_search,   states = NULL,   city = NULL,   limit = 1200L,   write_snapshot = TRUE,   snapshot_dir = NULL,   notify = TRUE )

title: Search NPI Database by Taxonomy — mysterycall_search_taxonomy
text: taxonomy_to_search character vector containing taxonomy description(s) search . states Optional character vector two-letter US state abbreviations (e.g. c("CO", "CA", "NY")). supplied search repeated state providers captured even large specialties exceed 1\,200-record per-query limit. Pass 50 state abbreviations perform complete national search. Defaults NULL (national search, capped limit records). city Optional city name passed npi::npi_search(). limit Maximum records request per API call. Capped 1\,200 NPI API. Defaults 1200L. write_snapshot Logical. TRUE, retrieved data saved .rds file later reference. Defaults TRUE. snapshot_dir Directory snapshot files written write_snapshot TRUE. Defaults session-specific folder inside tempdir() supplied. notify Logical. TRUE, play notification sound processing completes (requires optional beepr package). Defaults TRUE.

title: Search NPI Database by Taxonomy — mysterycall_search_taxonomy
text: data frame NPPES records matching requested taxonomy. Key columns include npi, first_name, last_name, taxonomies_desc (broad NPPES specialty label), taxonomies_primary (logical).

title: Search NPI Database by Taxonomy — mysterycall_search_taxonomy
text: taxonomies_desc reflects NPPES taxonomy code provider self- reported NPI registry. use column assign subspecialty. NPPES taxonomy codes reliably distinguish subspecialties (e.g. Neurotology vs. General Otolaryngology). Subspecialty must derived board certification data , using mysterycall_parse_certification_subspecialty() ABOHNS certification_type field, reconciled mysterycall_reconcile_specialty().

title: Search NPI Database by Taxonomy — mysterycall_search_taxonomy
text: function returns curated subset npi::npi_flatten() output. Retained (renaming): npi, first_name, last_name, middle_name, credential, addresses_city, addresses_state, addresses_postal_code, addresses_telephone_number, addresses_country_name, taxonomies_desc, taxonomies_primary, search_term. columns returned npi_flatten() explicit drop list also retained. Always dropped regardless NPI API returns: endpoints_* fields (electronic health endpoint metadata) other_names_* fields (former alternate name records) identifiers_* fields (legacy identifier records) Supplementary address fields: addresses_address_2, addresses_fax_number, addresses_address_type, addresses_country_code Basic metadata: basic_last_updated, basic_status, basic_name_prefix, basic_name_suffix, basic_certification_date Secondary taxonomy fields: taxonomies_code, taxonomies_taxonomy_group, taxonomies_state, taxonomies_license Internal working column: credential_lower access dropped columns, call npi::npi_search() npi::npi_flatten() directly filtering.

title: Search NPI Database by Taxonomy — mysterycall_search_taxonomy
text: Inputs: taxonomy_to_search must exact NUCC taxonomy description string (see mysterycall::taxonomy dataset valid values). NPI registry API must reachable; function retries 3 times exponential back-returning empty data frame. Guarantees: Returns zero-row data frame (never NULL) records found. Output rows deduplicated NPI. write_snapshot = TRUE, .rds file written output_dir reproducible re-runs without re-querying live API. Fails : Network unavailable retries (returns empty data frame, error). states contains invalid two-letter abbreviation (silently skipped).

title: Search NPI Database by Taxonomy — mysterycall_search_taxonomy
text: O(t * p / 200) HTTP requests t = number state batches p = max_records (default 1,200). page request targets < 200 rows. Full national search across 50 states one taxonomy takes ~2-5 min depending registry load. Results cached -memory per session use_cache = TRUE.

title: Search NPI Database by Taxonomy — mysterycall_search_taxonomy
text: mysterycall_run_workflow() mysterycall_search_and_process_npi() (indirectly via taxonomy lookup)

title: Search NPI Database by Taxonomy — mysterycall_search_taxonomy
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() # National search (limited to 1200 records per taxonomy): go_data <- mysterycall_search_taxonomy("Gynecologic Oncology")  # Full national search by looping over every state: all_states <- c(   "AL","AK","AZ","AR","CA","CO","CT","DE","FL","GA",   "HI","ID","IL","IN","IA","KS","KY","LA","ME","MD",   "MA","MI","MN","MS","MO","MT","NE","NV","NH","NJ",   "NM","NY","NC","ND","OH","OK","OR","PA","RI","SC",   "SD","TN","TX","UT","VT","VA","WA","WV","WI","WY" ) fpmrs_data <- mysterycall_search_taxonomy(   "Female Pelvic Medicine and Reconstructive Surgery",   states = all_states ) }

title: Split data into multiple parts and save each part as separate Excel files — mysterycall_split_and_save
code: mysterycall_split_and_save(   data_or_path,   output_directory,   lab_assistant_names,   seed = 1978,   complete_file_prefix = "complete_non_split_version_",   split_file_prefix = "",   recursive_create = TRUE,   insurance_order = c("Medicaid", "Blue Cross/Blue Shield") )

title: Split data into multiple parts and save each part as separate Excel files — mysterycall_split_and_save
text: data_or_path Either dataframe containing input data path input data file (RDS, CSV, Parquet, XLS/XLSX). output_directory Directory output Excel files saved. lab_assistant_names Vector lab assistant names name output files. seed Seed value randomization (default 1978). complete_file_prefix Prefix complete output file name (default "complete_non_split_version_"). split_file_prefix Prefix split output file name (default empty). recursive_create Logical indicating directories created recursively (default TRUE). insurance_order Vector insurance types ordered priority call scheduling (default c("Medicaid", "Blue Cross/Blue Shield")).

title: Split data into multiple parts and save each part as separate Excel files — mysterycall_split_and_save
text: invisible() character vector absolute file paths created Excel files. Element 1 combined "callers" workbook; subsequent elements per-assistant split workbooks order lab_assistant_names. Capture paths <- mysterycall_split_and_save(...) use paths downstream.

title: Split data into multiple parts and save each part as separate Excel files — mysterycall_split_and_save
text: Inputs: lab_assistant_names must \(\geq\) 2 entries; function errors one name provided. seed controls random assignment rows assistants; seed always produces split. output_directory created absent. Guarantees: Every input row appears exactly one assistant's workbook (row duplication, silent omission). Workbooks sorted insurance_order Medicaid rows appear first. combined "callers" workbook written addition individual files. Disaster Prevention: Prevents unequal workload distribution using round-robin row assignment rather random block sampling, can produce highly skewed splits small rosters.

title: Split data into multiple parts and save each part as separate Excel files — mysterycall_split_and_save
text: O(n log n) due sorting. Excel /O via openxlsx dominates large rosters; expect ~2-5 s 500 rows.

text: mysterycall_run_workflow()

what: Build a Table 1 for mystery caller studies — mysterycall_table1
title: Build a Table 1 for mystery caller studies — mysterycall_table1
text: Produces publication-ready summary table describing physician study characteristics, optionally stratified grouping variable (typically "insurance"). Continuous variables summarised median [IQR] /mean (SD); categorical variables n (%). Statistical tests chosen automatically: Wilcoxon rank-sum Kruskal-Wallis continuous variables, chi-square Fisher's exact categorical.

title: Build a Table 1 for mystery caller studies — mysterycall_table1
code: mysterycall_table1(   data,   covariates,   stratify_by = NULL,   include_overall = TRUE,   cont_stats = c("median_iqr", "mean_sd"),   digits = 1L,   p_value = TRUE,   min_cell = 5L,   variable_labels = NULL )

title: Build a Table 1 for mystery caller studies — mysterycall_table1
text: data data frame, typically output mysterycall_clean_phase1() merged genderize results. covariates Character vector column names include rows. Numeric columns treated continuous; others categorical. stratify_by Optional single column name used stratifying variable (e.g. "insurance"). NULL (default) single Overall column produced hypothesis tests. include_overall Logical. TRUE (default) Overall column included even stratify_by set. cont_stats Character vector controlling statistics shown numeric variables. Valid choices "median_iqr" (default, first) "mean_sd". can supplied show two rows per variable. digits Number decimal places continuous statistics percentages. Default 1. p_value Logical. TRUE (default) stratify_by set, p_value column appended (Wilcoxon/Kruskal-Wallis continuous, chi-square/Fisher's exact categorical). min_cell Minimum expected cell count Fisher's exact test used instead chi-square (2-group categorical ). Default 5. variable_labels Optional named character vector mapping column names display labels, e.g. c(wait_days = "Wait time (days)"). Unlabelled columns use column name.

title: Build a Table 1 for mystery caller studies — mysterycall_table1
text: list class mysterycall_table1 : table Tibble columns variable, level, Overall (include_overall), one column per stratum, p_value (p_value = TRUE stratified). row one statistic one category level. column_ns Named integer vector sample sizes per column. stratify_by name stratifying column, NULL. n Total rows data.

title: Build a Table 1 for mystery caller studies — mysterycall_table1
code: df <- data.frame(   gender    = c("Male", "Female", "Female", "Male", "Male", "Female"),   academic  = c("University", "Private Practice", "Private Practice",                 "University", "Private Practice", "University"),   wait_days = c(10, 25, 14, 30, 7, 21),   insurance = c("Medicaid", "BCBS", "Medicaid", "BCBS", "Medicaid", "BCBS") ) result <- mysterycall_table1(   df,   covariates   = c("gender", "academic", "wait_days"),   stratify_by  = "insurance" ) result$table #> # A tibble: 6 × 6 #>   variable  level            Overall       `BCBS (N=3)` `Medicaid (N=3)` p_value #>   <chr>     <chr>            <chr>         <chr>        <chr>            <chr>   #> 1 gender    Female           3 (50.0%)     2 (66.7%)    1 (33.3%)        1.000   #> 2 gender    Male             3 (50.0%)     1 (33.3%)    2 (66.7%)        NA      #> 3 academic  Private Practice 3 (50.0%)     1 (33.3%)    2 (66.7%)        1.000   #> 4 academic  University       3 (50.0%)     2 (66.7%)    1 (33.3%)        NA      #> 5 wait_days Median [IQR]     17.5 [11.0-2… 25.0 [23.0-… 10.0 [8.5-12.0]  0.081   #> 6 wait_days Mean (SD)        17.8 (9.0)    25.3 (4.5)   10.3 (3.5)       NA

title: Publication-ready Table 1 via gtsummary — mysterycall_table1_gtsummary
text: Wraps gtsummary::tbl_summary() opinionated defaults suitable mystery-caller study demographics: "Unknown" rows (use missing = "ifany" re-enable), bold row labels, overall column p-values stratified.

title: Publication-ready Table 1 via gtsummary — mysterycall_table1_gtsummary
code: mysterycall_table1_gtsummary(   data,   vars,   strata_col = NULL,   label_list = NULL,   missing = "no",   percent = c("column", "row", "cell"),   overall_last = FALSE,   ... )

title: Publication-ready Table 1 via gtsummary — mysterycall_table1_gtsummary
text: data data frame. vars Non-empty character vector column names include rows. names must present data. strata_col appears silently removed warning. strata_col Optional single character column name stratify (e.g. "insurance"). provided, Overall column p-values added automatically. label_list Optional named list mapping column names display labels, forwarded gtsummary::tbl_summary(). missing One "" (default), "ifany", "always". Controls missing-data reporting. Forwarded gtsummary::tbl_summary(). percent One "column" (default), "row", "cell". Controls denominator used categorical-variable percentages. overall_last Logical. strata_col provided, Overall column appear last (TRUE) first (FALSE, default)? ... Additional arguments forwarded gtsummary::tbl_summary().

title: Publication-ready Table 1 via gtsummary — mysterycall_table1_gtsummary
text: gtsummary tbl_summary object. Chain additional modifiers (e.g. gtsummary::modify_caption(), gtsummary::modify_spanning_header()) converting gtsummary::as_gt() gtsummary::as_flex_table().

title: Publication-ready Table 1 via gtsummary — mysterycall_table1_gtsummary
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() && requireNamespace("gtsummary", quietly = TRUE) df <- data.frame(   gender    = sample(c("Male", "Female"), 100, replace = TRUE),   setting   = sample(c("Academic", "Private"), 100, replace = TRUE),   age_cat   = sample(c("30-39", "40-49", "50-59"), 100, replace = TRUE),   insurance = sample(c("BCBS", "Medicaid"), 100, replace = TRUE) ) tbl <- mysterycall_table1_gtsummary(   df,   vars       = c("gender", "setting", "age_cat"),   strata_col = "insurance" ) tbl }

what: Most frequent level(s) of a categorical variable with percentage — mysterycall_table_percentages
title: Most frequent level(s) of a categorical variable with percentage — mysterycall_table_percentages
text: Counts level categorical variable, computes share non-missing rows, returns level(s) highest count. multiple levels tie top count, tied levels returned. NA values excluded counts denominator.

title: Most frequent level(s) of a categorical variable with percentage — mysterycall_table_percentages
code: mysterycall_table_percentages(data_frame, variable)

title: Most frequent level(s) of a categorical variable with percentage — mysterycall_table_percentages
text: data_frame data frame containing categorical variable. variable character string giving column name categorical variable within data_frame.

title: Most frequent level(s) of a categorical variable with percentage — mysterycall_table_percentages
text: data frame one row per level tied highest count. Columns: variable , n (count), percent (exact percentage non-missing rows, rounded).

title: Most frequent level(s) of a categorical variable with percentage — mysterycall_table_percentages
code: # "A" is most frequent: returns 1 row data_frame <- data.frame(category = c("A", "B", "A", "C", "A", "B", "B", "A")) mysterycall_table_percentages(data_frame, "category") #>   category n percent #> 1        A 4      50  # Three-way tie: all three returned df_tie <- data.frame(category = c("A", "B", "A", "B", "C", "C", "C", "A", "B")) mysterycall_table_percentages(df_tie, "category") #>   category n  percent #> 1        A 3 33.33333 #> 2        B 3 33.33333 #> 3        C 3 33.33333  # NAs are excluded from counts and the denominator df_na <- data.frame(category = c("A", NA, "A", "C", "A", "B", "B", NA)) mysterycall_table_percentages(df_na, "category") #>   category n percent #> 1        A 3      50

what: Calculate the Proportion of Each Level in a Categorical Variable — mysterycall_table_proportion
title: Calculate the Proportion of Each Level in a Categorical Variable — mysterycall_table_proportion
text: function calculates proportion level specified categorical variable within data frame. returns data frame counts percentages level.

title: Calculate the Proportion of Each Level in a Categorical Variable — mysterycall_table_proportion
code: mysterycall_table_proportion(data, variable_name)

title: Calculate the Proportion of Each Level in a Categorical Variable — mysterycall_table_proportion
text: data data frame containing categorical variable. variable_name name categorical variable proportions calculated, passed unquoted expression.

title: Calculate the Proportion of Each Level in a Categorical Variable — mysterycall_table_proportion
text: data frame one row per unique non-missing level variable_name. Columns: variable , n (count), percent (percentage total, rounded 2 decimal places).

title: Calculate the Proportion of Each Level in a Categorical Variable — mysterycall_table_proportion
text: variable_name uses tidy evaluation ({{ }}), pass column name unquoted (e.g. mysterycall_table_proportion(df, gender)). Use mysterycall_table_percentages() column name string.

title: Calculate the Proportion of Each Level in a Categorical Variable — mysterycall_table_proportion
code: # Example 1: Basic usage with a simple dataset data <- data.frame(gender = c("Male", "Female", "Female", "Male", "Male", "Female")) result <- mysterycall_table_proportion(data, gender) print(result) #>   gender n percent #> 1 Female 3      50 #> 2   Male 3      50  # Example 2: Handling a dataset with missing values df_na <- data.frame(gender = c("Male", NA, "Female", "Female", "Male", "Female", NA)) result <- mysterycall_table_proportion(df_na, gender) print(result) #>   gender n percent #> 1 Female 3      60 #> 2   Male 2      40  # Example 3: Using a variable with multiple levels df_multi <- data.frame(grade = c("A", "B", "A", "C", "B", "A", "C", "B")) result <- mysterycall_table_proportion(df_multi, grade) print(result) #>   grade n percent #> 1     A 3    37.5 #> 2     B 3    37.5 #> 3     C 2    25.0

what: Validate and Remove Invalid NPI Numbers — mysterycall_validate_npi
title: Validate and Remove Invalid NPI Numbers — mysterycall_validate_npi
text: function reads CSV file containing NPI numbers, validates format using npi package, removes rows missing invalid NPIs.

title: Validate and Remove Invalid NPI Numbers — mysterycall_validate_npi
code: mysterycall_validate_npi(input_data)

title: Validate and Remove Invalid NPI Numbers — mysterycall_validate_npi
text: input_data Either data frame containing NPI numbers path CSV file.

title: Validate and Remove Invalid NPI Numbers — mysterycall_validate_npi
text: data frame columns input_data, plus logical column npi_is_valid.  rows non-empty, 10-digit, Luhn-valid NPI values returned; npi column always character type.  Returns zero-row data frame (preserving column names) rows pass validation.

title: Validate and Remove Invalid NPI Numbers — mysterycall_validate_npi
text: Inputs: input_data must contain npi column (character numeric). NPIs validated via Luhn checksum algorithm (NPI standard). Guarantees: Output rows \(\subseteq\) input rows  –  rows removed, never added. Every removed row either NA, non-10-digit string, failed Luhn checksum. Output NPI column always character type. Disaster Prevention: Prevents silent retention placeholder NPIs (e.g., -zeros test values "1234567890") match real providers downstream joins inflate match rates.

title: Validate and Remove Invalid NPI Numbers — mysterycall_validate_npi
text: O(n) small constant; Luhn validation pure arithmetic check. Expect < 0.1 s 10,000 rows.

title: Validate and Remove Invalid NPI Numbers — mysterycall_validate_npi
text: mysterycall_run_workflow() mysterycall_search_and_process_npi()

title: Validate and Remove Invalid NPI Numbers — mysterycall_validate_npi
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() df <- data.frame(npi = c("1234567893", "0000000000", NA_character_)) mysterycall_validate_npi(df) }

what: Validate parsed physician names for quality issues — mysterycall_validate_parsed_names
title: Validate parsed physician names for quality issues — mysterycall_validate_parsed_names

title: Validate parsed physician names for quality issues — mysterycall_validate_parsed_names
code: mysterycall_validate_parsed_names(   parsed_names,   require_first = TRUE,   require_last = TRUE )

title: Validate parsed physician names for quality issues — mysterycall_validate_parsed_names
text: parsed_names tibble mysterycall_parse_physician_name(), least columns first_name, last_name, middle_name, parse_confidence. require_first Logical. TRUE (default) rows first_name NA marked is_valid = FALSE.  Set FALSE data sources store last names (e.g. single-name lookup tables). require_last Logical. TRUE (default) rows last_name NA marked is_valid = FALSE.  Set FALSE processing mononyms incomplete name strings absence surname expected.

title: Validate parsed physician names for quality issues — mysterycall_validate_parsed_names
text: input tibble additional columns: has_first, has_last, has_middle Logical presence flags. valid_first, valid_last Logical – pass require_* gate. is_valid valid_first & valid_last. last_is_credential Logical – last name looks like "MD", "PHD", etc. last_is_suffix Logical – last name looks like "Jr", "III", etc. last_too_short Logical – last name 1-2 chars (excluding common short surnames "", "Li", "Ng", "Wu"). middle_has_particle Logical – middle name contains name particle ("de", "van", "von", etc.) may belong last name. quality_issue First detected quality issue code, NA.

title: Validate parsed physician names for quality issues — mysterycall_validate_parsed_names
code: parsed <- mysterycall_parse_physician_name(c("Smith, John, Jr.", "MD")) validated <- mysterycall_validate_parsed_names(parsed) validated$is_valid       # c(TRUE, FALSE) #> [1]  TRUE FALSE validated$quality_issue  # c(NA, "last_name_is_credential") #> [1] NA NA

what: Validate North-American (NANP) phone number strings — mysterycall_validate_phone
title: Validate North-American (NANP) phone number strings — mysterycall_validate_phone
text: Strips non-digit characters, applies North American Numbering Plan (NANP) syntactic rules, looks area code's home state, optionally checks whether state matches physician's practice state.

title: Validate North-American (NANP) phone number strings — mysterycall_validate_phone
code: mysterycall_validate_phone(phone_str, practice_state = NULL, nanp_path = NULL)

title: Validate North-American (NANP) phone number strings — mysterycall_validate_phone
text: phone_str Character vector phone strings (NAs allowed). Accepts common formats: "(NPA) NXX-XXXX", "NPA-NXX-XXXX", bare 10 digits, "1NPANXXXXXX". practice_state Character vector 2-letter US state postal codes, length phone_str length 1 (recycled).  Pass NULL skip state-matching check (phone_area_code_matches_state entries NA). nanp_path Optional path custom NANP lookup CSV.  NULL (default) bundled inst/extdata/nanp_area_codes_us.csv used.

title: Validate North-American (NANP) phone number strings — mysterycall_validate_phone
text: data frame one row per element phone_str columns: phone_e164_valid Logical. TRUE number passes NANP syntactic rules. phone_npa Character. 3-digit North American Numbering Plan area code, NA_character_ number syntactically invalid. phone_state_from_npa Character. 2-letter US postal abbreviation area code's registered state, NA_character_ area code absent bundled lookup table. phone_area_code_matches_state Logical. TRUE phone_state_from_npa exactly matches practice_state; FALSE differ; NA practice_state NULL. phone_validity_flag Character. One "valid", "missing", "invalid_format", "unknown_area_code", "area_code_state_mismatch".

title: Validate North-American (NANP) phone number strings — mysterycall_validate_phone
text: Syntactic validity rules applied: Exactly 10 digits stripping non-digits (optional leading "1" country code dropped first). NPA first digit (N) 2-9. NXX first digit (N) 2-9. NXX N11 code (e.g. 911, 411, 211). Area-code--state mapping loaded bundled inst/extdata/nanp_area_codes_us.csv file.  Pass nanp_path override custom CSV columns area_code (integer) state (2-letter US postal abbreviation).

title: Validate North-American (NANP) phone number strings — mysterycall_validate_phone
code: mysterycall_validate_phone(   c("(303) 555-1212", "555-1212", NA, "800-555-0199"),   practice_state = "CO" )

title: Validate North-American (NANP) phone number strings — mysterycall_validate_phone
code: mysterycall_validate_phone(   c("(303) 555-1212", "555-1212", NA),   practice_state = "CO" ) #>   phone_e164_valid phone_npa phone_state_from_npa phone_area_code_matches_state #> 1             TRUE       303                   CO                          TRUE #> 2            FALSE      <NA>                 <NA>                         FALSE #> 3            FALSE      <NA>                 <NA>                         FALSE #>   phone_validity_flag #> 1               valid #> 2      invalid_format #> 3             missing  # Without state matching mysterycall_validate_phone(c("2125551234", "0005551234"), practice_state = NULL) #>   phone_e164_valid phone_npa phone_state_from_npa phone_area_code_matches_state #> 1             TRUE       212                   NY                            NA #> 2            FALSE       000                 <NA>                            NA #>   phone_validity_flag #> 1               valid #> 2      invalid_format

what: Verify the content-addressable identity of an audit trail JSON file — mysterycall_verify_artifact
title: Verify the content-addressable identity of an audit trail JSON file — mysterycall_verify_artifact
text: Loads audit JSON written mysterycall_clean_phase1(), strips volatile fields, recomputes SHA-256 digest stable payload, compares stored artifact_id. mismatch means file modified written artifact_id computed different logic.

title: Verify the content-addressable identity of an audit trail JSON file — mysterycall_verify_artifact
code: mysterycall_verify_artifact(audit_path)

title: Verify the content-addressable identity of an audit trail JSON file — mysterycall_verify_artifact
text: audit_path Character scalar. Path audit_trail_*.json file.

title: Verify the content-addressable identity of an audit trail JSON file — mysterycall_verify_artifact
text: Invisibly returns TRUE verification passes. Throws informative error verification fails file parsed.

title: Verify the content-addressable identity of an audit trail JSON file — mysterycall_verify_artifact
text: Inputs: audit_path must point readable JSON file produced mysterycall_clean_phase1() schema version >= 1.2.0. Guarantees: Returns TRUE (invisibly) artifact_id matches recomputed digest. outcome raises error diagnostic message. Never modifies audit file. Fails : audit_path exist valid JSON. file produced schema version prior 1.2.0 (artifact_id). recomputed digest match stored artifact_id.

title: Verify the content-addressable identity of an audit trail JSON file — mysterycall_verify_artifact
text: Downstream validation scripts CI pipelines

title: Verify the content-addressable identity of an audit trail JSON file — mysterycall_verify_artifact
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() # Verify an audit file written by clean_phase1 mysterycall_verify_artifact("path/to/audit_trail_2026-05-09.json") }

title: Summarise appointment wait times — mysterycall_wait_time_summary
text: Computes descriptive statistics (n, mean, SD, median, IQR, range) numeric wait-time column runs appropriate non-parametric test groups specified: Wilcoxon rank-sum two groups, Kruskal-Wallis three .

title: Summarise appointment wait times — mysterycall_wait_time_summary
code: mysterycall_wait_time_summary(   data,   wait_col = "wait_days",   group_by = NULL,   conf_level = 0.95 )

title: Summarise appointment wait times — mysterycall_wait_time_summary
text: data data frame containing least column named wait_col. wait_col Name numeric column holding days appointment. Defaults "wait_days". group_by Optional character scalar naming grouping column (e.g. "insurance"). NULL (default) statistics computed full dataset test run. conf_level Confidence level used Wilcoxon test. Default 0.95.

title: Summarise appointment wait times — mysterycall_wait_time_summary
text: named list : summary Tibble one row per group (one row overall). group_by non-NULL, first column named grouping variable (character). Remaining columns: n (integer), n_missing (integer), mean (numeric), sd (numeric), median (numeric), q1 (numeric), q3 (numeric), min (numeric), max (numeric). test htest object stats::wilcox.test() (two groups) stats::kruskal.test() (three groups), NULL test run (.e., group_by = NULL). p_value Numeric p-value extracted test, NA_real_ test run. test_name Character. One "Wilcoxon rank-sum", "Kruskal-Wallis", "none". interpretation Character. One-sentence plain-language summary suitable pasting Results section.

title: Summarise appointment wait times — mysterycall_wait_time_summary
text: Wait times must already numeric (days appointment). Phase 2 data records appointment date rather count, compute data$wait_days <- .numeric(data$appt_date - data$call_date) calling function.

title: Summarise appointment wait times — mysterycall_wait_time_summary
code: df <- data.frame(   wait_days = c(3, 7, 14, 21, 5, 10, 30, 2),   insurance = c("Medicaid", "Medicaid", "Medicaid", "Medicaid",                 "BCBS", "BCBS", "BCBS", "BCBS") ) mysterycall_wait_time_summary(df, group_by = "insurance") #> $summary #> # A tibble: 2 × 10 #>   insurance     n n_missing  mean    sd median    q1    q3   min   max #>   <chr>     <int>     <int> <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 BCBS          4         0  11.8 12.6     7.5  4.25  15       2    30 #> 2 Medicaid      4         0  11.2  7.93   10.5  6     15.8     3    21 #>  #> $test #>  #> 	Wilcoxon rank sum test with continuity correction #>  #> data:  gdata[[1L]] and gdata[[2L]] #> W = 7, p-value = 0.8852 #> alternative hypothesis: true location shift is not equal to 0 #>  #>  #> $p_value #> [1] 0.8852339 #>  #> $test_name #> [1] "Wilcoxon rank-sum (Mann-Whitney U)" #>  #> $interpretation #> [1] "BCBS: median 7.5 days (IQR 4.25-15, n=4); Medicaid: median 10.5 days (IQR 6-15.75, n=4). Wilcoxon rank-sum (Mann-Whitney U): p = 0.885" #>

what: Generate a results paragraph for a mystery caller Poisson model — mysterycall_write_results_paragraph
title: Generate a results paragraph for a mystery caller Poisson model — mysterycall_write_results_paragraph
text: Produces ready--paste results sentence describing IRRs multivariable Poisson regression, formatted clinical manuscripts.

title: Generate a results paragraph for a mystery caller Poisson model — mysterycall_write_results_paragraph
code: mysterycall_write_results_paragraph(   model_result,   ref_group,   exposure_col,   outcome_label = "appointment acceptance",   alpha = 0.05,   irr_digits = 2L,   ci_digits = 2L,   p_digits = 3L )

title: Generate a results paragraph for a mystery caller Poisson model — mysterycall_write_results_paragraph
text: model_result Either mysterycall_poisson_model object (element $irr_table) data frame columns term, irr, ci_lower, ci_upper, p_value. ref_group Character scalar: reference group label (e.g. "commercial insurance"). exposure_col Character scalar: name exposure variable whose terms described (e.g. "insurance"). outcome_label Character scalar: human-readable outcome label. Default "appointment acceptance". alpha Numeric: significance level. Default 0.05. (Currently reserved future use.) irr_digits Integer: decimal places IRR. Default 2L. ci_digits Integer: decimal places CI bounds. Default 2L. p_digits Integer: decimal places p-value. Default 3L.

title: Generate a results paragraph for a mystery caller Poisson model — mysterycall_write_results_paragraph
text: single character string containing result sentences.

title: Generate a results paragraph for a mystery caller Poisson model — mysterycall_write_results_paragraph
code: irr_tbl <- data.frame(   term     = c("(Intercept)", "insuranceMedicaid", "insuranceUninsured"),   irr      = c(1.0, 0.72, 0.55),   ci_lower = c(NA,  0.60, 0.40),   ci_upper = c(NA,  0.86, 0.75),   p_value  = c(NA,  0.0003, 0.0000001),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) mysterycall_write_results_paragraph(irr_tbl, "commercial insurance", "insurance") #> [1] "In multivariable Poisson regression, insurance was significantly associated with appointment acceptance (see Table X). Compared with commercial insurance, callers presenting as Medicaid had an IRR of 0.72 (95% CI 0.60-0.86; p < 0.001) for appointment acceptance. Compared with commercial insurance, callers presenting as Uninsured had an IRR of 0.55 (95% CI 0.40-0.75; p < 0.001) for appointment acceptance."

title: Green Journal ggplot2 theme — theme_green_journal
text: Publication-quality theme conforming Obstetrics & Gynecology (Green Journal, Wolters Kluwer) 2024 author guidelines: white background, Arial/sans font, black axes tick marks, minimal gridlines, bottom legend. Pair palette_green_journal() save_green_journal_figure() complete submission workflow.

title: Green Journal ggplot2 theme — theme_green_journal
code: theme_green_journal(base_size = 10, base_family = "Arial")  theme_publication(base_size = 10, base_family = "Arial")

title: Green Journal ggplot2 theme — theme_green_journal
text: base_size Numeric. Base font size points (default 10; Green Journal range 8-12 pt, minimum 6 pt labels). base_family Character. Font family. Defaults "Arial" automatic fallback "sans" Arial unavailable.

title: Green Journal ggplot2 theme — theme_green_journal
text: ggplot2::theme() object.

title: Green Journal map theme — theme_green_journal_map
text: Variant theme_green_journal() choropleth maps, isochrone coverage maps, spatial figures. Removes axis elements, centers title, adds framed legend. Use ggplot2::coord_sf() mysterycall_crs_albers_conus() equal-area projection.

what: Utility helpers for mysterycall workflows — utility_library
title: Utility helpers for mysterycall workflows — utility_library
text: Collection helper functions streamline dependency checking, file management, logging, quality reporting across mysterycall projects. See individual help pages full documentation: mysterycall_check_dependencies — verify optionally   install package dependencies. mysterycall_resolve_path — normalize project-relative paths. mysterycall_check_data_completeness — compute column-level   completeness metrics. mysterycall_export_with_backup — write outputs   timestamped backups. mysterycall_use_quiet_logging — toggle quiet-mode logging. mysterycall_log_info — emit timestamped log message. mysterycall_standard_labels — canonical presentation labels. mysterycall_standard_palette — standard color palettes. mysterycall_quality_tier — map completeness score   quality tier.

title: mysterycall 1.3.0
text: Released 2026-05-08.

title: mysterycall 1.3.0
text: Package renamed tyler mysterycall. library(tyler) longer work; use library(mysterycall). 100 exported functions now carry mysterycall_ prefix (e.g., mysterycall_geocode(), mysterycall_search_and_process_npi()). previous tyler_ prefix names retained deprecated shims emit warning forward new name. even-older unprefixed names (e.g., check_normality()) continue work double-deprecated shims.

title: mysterycall 1.3.0
text: Data quality validation mysterycall_validate_phone() — validates US phone numbers NANP structural rules (NPA/NXX first-digit constraints, N11 service codes) optionally checks area code belongs provider’s reported practice state via bundled lookup table. Returns tidy data frame phone_e164_valid, phone_npa, phone_state_from_npa, phone_area_code_matches_state, phone_validity_flag columns. Fully vectorised; lookup table lazy-loaded cached per session. mysterycall_parse_physician_name() — converts free-text physician name strings (board certification data, NPPES, CMS sources) structured first / middle / last / suffix / title fields confidence scoring (high / medium / low) warning flags. Handles credential vs. Vietnamese surname disambiguation ("Robert Smith " → suffix ""; "Linda " → last name ""), three-part comma format ("Smith, John, Jr."), hyphenated names, name particles. mysterycall_validate_parsed_names() — extends parse output quality flags: has_first, has_last, is_valid, last_is_credential, last_is_suffix, last_too_short, middle_has_particle, quality_issue. mysterycall_format_physician_name() — reassembles parsed components "last_first", "first_last", "formal" display strings; vectorised. mysterycall_test_name_parser() — runs 13-case edge-case accuracy suite prints per-case report console. extended 30-case benchmark corpus available inst/extdata/name_benchmark_corpus.csv; evaluation script data-raw/benchmark_name_parser.R. Safe join wrappers mysterycall_safe_left_join() — wraps dplyr::left_join() key-type harmonisation, right-side uniqueness assertion, coverage threshold enforcement (min_coverage, default 0.98), row-multiplication guard (max_duplication, default 1.02×), optional CSV audit report. mysterycall_safe_inner_join() — wraps dplyr::inner_join() guards; default min_coverage = 0.90. mysterycall_safe_semi_join() — wraps dplyr::semi_join() keep-rate threshold; default min_coverage = 0.50. mysterycall_safe_anti_join() — wraps dplyr::anti_join() -exclusion cap (max_matched, default 1.0). mysterycall_assert_unique_keys() — asserts specified columns form unique key; optionally deduplicates (first row kept) instead erroring. Package infrastructure humaniformat moved Suggests → Imports; runtime requireNamespace() guard removed. 119 new test_that blocks across three new test files (test-validate-phone.R, test-parse-physician-name.R, test-join-safety.R). Five new vignettes: data-quality, statistical-analysis, provider-classification, workflow-orchestration, table-generation. CITATION.cff updated full abstract, keywords, affiliation. CONTRIBUTING.md expanded full developer guide.

title: mysterycall 1.3.0
text: Replaced 34 \dontrun{} blocks @examplesIf interactive(). Replaced 18 live print() calls message() invisible(). Moved sf Imports Suggests; added requireNamespace() guards 8 geospatial functions avoid forcing GDAL/GEOS/PROJ users. Bumped minimum R version 3.5.0 4.1.0. Removed duplicate Maintainer: field DESCRIPTION. Added GitHub issue/PR templates repostatus.org badge.

title: mysterycall 1.2.2
text: Released 2026-05-04.

title: mysterycall 1.2.2
text: library(tyler) longer crashes R causes system memory exhaustion. Seven heavy packages (ggmap, ggspatial, hereR, leaflet, leaflet.extras, lme4, censusapi) moved Imports Suggests compiled spatial libraries (GDAL, GEOS, PROJ) loaded relevant function first called, package attach. Two packages declared Imports never called (tigris, effects) removed entirely. create_isochrones() longer accumulates memoized results RAM indefinitely. internal memoization object now exposed mysterycall_clear_isochrone_cache(). Call processing large batch reclaim memory. create_isochrones_for_dataframe() create_individual_isochrone_plots() previously called beepr::beep() unconditionally even though beepr suggested package. calls now guarded requireNamespace().

title: mysterycall 1.2.2
text: states — loops state deduplicates NPI, bypassing NPI API’s hard 1,200-record-per-query cap. Pass 50 state abbreviations perform complete national search. city — optional city filter passed directly npi::npi_search(). limit — controls records per API call (max 1,200). mapping geospatial functions now emit clear stop() message exact install.packages() command required optional package installed.

title: mysterycall 1.2.1
text: Released 2025-10-23.

title: mysterycall 1.2.1
text: Released align metadata artifacts package website codemeta specification. Introduced Imotive News & Changelog vignette centralising release notes. Documented mysterycall_run_workflow() coordinates roster creation, validation, call preparation, QA Imotive projects.

title: mysterycall 1.2.1
text: mysterycall_not_contacted_states() now ignores rows without affirmative contact outcomes reports number unique physicians reached.

title: mysterycall 1.2.1
text: search_npi() → use mysterycall_search_and_process_npi() test_and_process_isochrones() → use mysterycall_isochrones_for_df() process_and_save_isochrones() → use mysterycall_isochrones_for_df()

title: mysterycall 0.0.0.9000
text: Added NEWS.md track changes. Verified R-CMD-check workflows macOS, Windows, Ubuntu. Moved provider Suggests; added runtime checks throughout. Refactored mysterycall_genderize() use Genderize.io API, removing dependency non-CRAN genderdata package. Added mysterycall_geocode() simplify geocoding lists addresses. Added vignette skeleton aggregating provider data.
